description_long: This gatehouse features a gabled roof covered in weathered, mossy tiles, giving it an aged and historic appearance. The structure is primarily built from rough-hewn stone blocks, with two prominent cylindrical columns at the front supporting a triangular pediment. The entryway is wide and open, framed by the columns and stonework, allowing easy passage through the center. The side walls are solid stone, and the roof overhangs slightly, providing some shelter at the entrance. The stonework is complemented by decorative trim and molding, particularly around the pediment and column capitals. There are no visible windows or doors, emphasizing its function as a passage or checkpoint rather than an enclosed building.
description: A stone gatehouse with a mossy gabled roof, open central passage, and two prominent front columns supporting a triangular pediment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone structure, open passage, and the gabled roof with visible moss and weathering.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone walls and the tiled roof, with minimal architectural detail visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two cylindrical columns, triangular pediment, and open entryway framed by stonework.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stone wall and roof tiles, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_entryway_design: Rectangular open stone entry framed by two cylindrical columns and a triangular pediment.
attribute_window_placement: No windows present on any side.
